children.text: We see a close-up shot "Fembot," who opens a curtain and knocks on the screen to catch the viewers' attention. The close up shot of the fembot creates a sense of intimacy. The knocking of the screen breaks the fourth wall which helps engage the viewers and makes them feel as if they are in that room with them. This creates a playful gesture which helps spark curiosity and humor with the viewers and helps them experience the scene more vividly. This introduces the audience to a party atmosphere and livens up the mood as soon as the opening scene hits.
Video zooms out to a wideshot of Fembot and now "Brobot," their bodies eject a bottle of Svedka vodka and a cocktail shaker

The video then shows the male robot pouring the svedka bottle to the svedka container that the female robot is holding. This shows human like interactiuons as they prepare to do something with the drink which is later shown. Music shifts.
Associates Svedka with fun, as seen with the various clips of people, and robots, dancing with a bottle in hand. Music changes to something more upbeat, party vibes.
Fembot dances with the shaker, depicted at the center of the dance floor; Svedka = center of attention
Fembot pours drink into Brobot's glass; Brobot drinks and it spills onto his wiring (<-- irony?)
Definitvely declares Svedka is a liquor brand. Liquor = familiar with party life. Tag line that emphasizes this association
